title: Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil
tagline: Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil is a temple of Shiva of Hindu tradition located in the town of Piravom, Ernakulam, Kerala, India.
overview.description: Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil is a temple of Shiva of Hindu tradition located in the town of Piravom, Ernakulam, Kerala, India. A unique phenomenon observed here is that the Muvattupuzha river flowing west from east changes direction on reaching the temple. It retraces its path and flows east for a certain distance before diverting again to north and west. Had the river not changed its course, the temple would have been washed away.

topPlaces.name: Pazhoor Padipura
topPlaces.description: Pazhoor Padippura, is situated 33 km towards the southeast of Kochi, near Piravom, Kerala. It is an astrology center linked to Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil temple.

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Piravom
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Piravom, also known as Piravom Valiyapally, is a prominent church that belongs to the Malankara Orthodox Syrian Church.

image.alt: St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Piravom

topPlaces.name: St. Mary's Simhasana Church, Veliyanad
topPlaces.description: St. Mary's Simhasana Church (Vadayaparambu simhasana Palli), Vadayaparambu is one of Kerala's prominent Syriac Orthodox churches.

topPlaces.name: Piravom
topPlaces.description: Piravom (Malayalam pronunciation: [piraʋəm]) is a town and municipality in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. It is located 30 km from Ernakulam, the nearest city and 22 km (13.7 mi) south of the district collectorate in Thrikkakara.The Piravom Road Railway Station is situated 9 km from the town.

topPlaces.name: Maneed
topPlaces.description: Maneed is a village/ rurban area in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Thirumarayoor
topPlaces.description: Thirumarayoor is a small village situated around 7 km from Piravom in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. A sub-post office of Arakunnam (PIN-682313) and a co-operative bank are the only public firms operating here.

topPlaces.name: Edakkattuvayal
topPlaces.description: Edakkattuvayal is a village in the Ernakulam district of Kerala, India. It is located in the Kanayannur taluk.

topPlaces.name: Onakkoor
topPlaces.description: Onakkoor is a village in Ernakulam district in the Indian state of Kerala. The main attractions in the village are Onakkoor River and Onakkoor Temple.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil?
faq.a: The best time to visit Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (31 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (22 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil?
faq.a: Stays in Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Pazhoor Perumthrikkovil?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pazhoor Padipura, St. Mary's Orthodox Syrian Cathedral, Piravom, St. Mary's Simhasana Church, Veliyanad, Piravom, Maneed.
